refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure end-to-end teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly include the latency metric and network conditions clarifies the performance requirement and safety context.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of teleoperation communication under defined network conditions using telemetry data during test scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in teleoperation data transmission.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link quality to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with latency monitoring.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency (e.g., UDP with priority scheduling).
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to reduce data path length.
DP3: Use dedicated network link with QoS guarantees.
DP4: Real-time telemetry and tasking data compression and prioritization.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency (e.g., UDP with priority scheduling).

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
Dependency on telemetry data format and tasking interface timing.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses the latency requirement with minimal coupling to other DPs; network link and telemetry are external factors.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed parameters for latency measurement.
Risk that protocol optimization alone may not achieve latency target if network link degrades.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP3 (dedicated network link) if latency targets are not met in testing.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for implementing communication protocols, telemetry, and tasking interfaces to achieve low latency teleoperation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection providing data transport with QoS.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time telemetry data stream sent to Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control inputs received from NGHE Solution or Site Manager.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of DP1 logic; low diffusion as protocol logic centralized here.
Vehicle module depends on telemetry but does not contain protocol logic.
Coordinates teleoperation but does not implement protocol stack.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing communication protocol optimization in Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact if Teleoperation module interfaces poorly with Network Link or Vehicle modules.
Risk of duplicated protocol logic if other modules implement overlapping functionality.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Enforce strict interface contracts and monitor latency metrics at module boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP directly addresses refined FR with clear latency optimization focus and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to centralized protocol logic in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as best ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry data and network monitoring at Teleoperation module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-related functionality to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Add monitoring hooks in Teleoperation module for latency verification.
Review network link QoS parameters in integration testing.

decision.justification: Current level decomposition is consistent, with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting latency and safety requirements.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in teleoperation data transmission.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link quality to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with latency monitoring.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement protocol optimizations effectively.
open_risks: Potential interface mismatches causing latency spikes.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under test network conditions.
Interface performance logs between Teleoperation and Network Link.
